plugin_desc: Pulse Secure / Ivanti Connect Secure (IVE) SSL VPN gateway-specific SNMP collection (PULSESECURE-PSG-MIB, enterprise PEN 12532). In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects the IVE system health scalars — CPU utilization (iveCpuUtil), memory utilization (iveMemoryUtil), swap utilization (iveSwapUtil), disk utilization (diskFullPercent) all reported directly as 0-100 percentages, chassis temperature (iveTemperature, degrees Celsius read directly; only MAG-series blades return a real value while other platforms return 0) and the number of signed-in web users (signedInWebUsers). Fan and power-supply objects are not exposed by the MIB, so those are N/A. Supported brands/models — Pulse Connect Secure / Ivanti Connect Secure, MAG / PSA series SSL VPN and ZTNA gateways (enterprise PEN 12532).
name: Firewall

metrics.name: snmp_uptime
metrics.description: This metric indicates the uptime of the device since the last restart. By monitoring the uptime, network administrators can understand the stability and reliability of the device and identify potential reboot or failure issues.

metrics.name: device_cpu_usage
metrics.description: CPU utilization of the Pulse Secure / Ivanti Connect Secure IVE system (PULSESECURE-PSG-MIB iveCpuUtil, OID 12532.10), reported directly as a 0-100 percentage. Sustained high CPU usage can indicate VPN session overload, SSL/TLS processing pressure, or attack activity affecting tunnel setup and management responsiveness.

metrics.name: interface_ifAdminStatus
metrics.description: This metric indicates the administrative status of the network interface.

metrics.name: interface_ifOperStatus
metrics.description: This metric reflects the actual operational status of the network interface.

metrics.name: interface_ifSpeed
metrics.description: This metric indicates the maximum data transmission speed supported by the network interface.

metrics.name: interface_ifInErrors
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of incoming error packets over the past 5 minutes.

metrics.name: interface_ifOutErrors
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of outgoing error packets over the past 5 minutes.

metrics.name: interface_ifInDiscards
metrics.description: This metric represents the average rate of incoming discarded packets over the past 5 minutes.

metrics.name: interface_ifOutDiscards
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of outgoing discarded packets over the past 5 minutes.

metrics.name: interface_ifInUcastPkts
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of incoming unicast packets over the past 5 minutes.

metrics.name: interface_ifOutUcastPkts
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of outgoing unicast packets over the past 5 minutes.

metrics.name: interface_ifInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes.

metrics.name: interface_ifOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, based on the 64-bit ifHCInOctets counter which does not wrap on high-speed interfaces.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, based on the 64-bit ifHCOutOctets counter which does not wrap on high-speed interfaces.

metrics.name: device_total_incoming_traffic
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of total bytes received by the entire device over the past 5 minutes.

metrics.name: device_total_outgoing_traffic
metrics.description: This metric represents the average rate of total bytes sent by the entire device over the past 5 minutes.

metrics.name: device_memory_usage
metrics.description: Memory utilization of the Pulse Secure / Ivanti Connect Secure IVE system (PULSESECURE-PSG-MIB iveMemoryUtil, OID 12532.11), reported directly as a 0-100 percentage. High memory pressure can cause the gateway to refuse new VPN sessions, so investigate session counts or abnormal load promptly.

metrics.name: device_swap_usage
metrics.description: Swap utilization of the Pulse Secure / Ivanti Connect Secure IVE system (PULSESECURE-PSG-MIB iveSwapUtil, OID 12532.24), reported directly as a 0-100 percentage. Sustained swap usage indicates memory exhaustion and degrades VPN session performance.

metrics.name: device_disk_usage
metrics.description: Disk-space utilization of the Pulse Secure / Ivanti Connect Secure IVE system (PULSESECURE-PSG-MIB diskFullPercent, OID 12532.25), reported directly as a 0-100 percentage. A nearly full disk can prevent logging and configuration changes and may destabilize the gateway.

metrics.name: device_temperature_celsius
metrics.description: Chassis temperature in degrees Celsius of the Pulse Secure / Ivanti Connect Secure device (PULSESECURE-PSG-MIB iveTemperature, OID 12532.42), read directly with no scaling. Only MAG-series blades report a real value; other platforms return 0. High temperatures can indicate cooling failure and risk thermal damage.

metrics.name: device_web_users
metrics.description: Number of currently signed-in web users on the Pulse Secure / Ivanti Connect Secure gateway (PULSESECURE-PSG-MIB signedInWebUsers, OID 12532.2). Tracking the active session count helps capacity planning and detecting abnormal connection spikes.
